name: Window Place
paraphrase: In every room where you spend any length of time during the day, make at least one window into a 'window place': a deliberately shaped zone next to a window that combines a generous view, a seat, and some enclosure. Alexander argues that a room without such a place forces occupants to choose between sitting comfortably and being drawn toward the light, leaving the room unsettled; a proper window place resolves the tension so people naturally come to rest there.
